channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Grupo de economistas e políticos quer Leite na corrida presidencial
item.description: A group of economists, former parliamentarians, and civic leaders is organizing a manifesto urging Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite to run for president in 2026 under a different party, after being excluded from the presidential race by his own party. The effort, reported by Folha de S.Paulo, aims to overcome the party veto and secure Leite’s participation in the election.

item.title: Lula critica privatização da BR Distribuidora e diz que ‘atravessadores’ impedem queda no preço do diesel
item.description: President Lula criticized the 2021 privatization of BR Distribuidora by the Bolsonaro government, stating that 'middlemen' prevent diesel price reductions from Petrobras from reaching consumers. He said the government is using regulatory bodies like ANP, Senacon, and the Federal Police to combat abusive fuel price hikes, citing the 'Vem Diesel' operation which issued 16 infringement notices. Lula also blamed the U.S. and Israel for the global diesel price surge due to their attacks on Iran, asserting that Brazil should not be a victim of foreign conflicts.

item.title: RJ: Flávio anuncia apoio a Ruas e movimentos sociais pedem eleição direta
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) announced his support for the pre-candidacy of state deputy Douglas Ruas (PL) for governor of Rio de Janeiro, regardless of the electoral method. Meanwhile, the MNU and the Popular Movement of Favelas advocate for a direct election for governor.

item.title: Fachin quer fim do inquérito das fake news e votar Código de Ética em 2026
item.description: Supreme Court President Edson Fachin announced he intends to vote on the proposed Ethics Code for the STF still in 2024. He also expressed concern over the fake news inquiry and advocated for discussing its closure.

item.title: Paulinho da Força descarta apoiar Lula em outubro
item.description: Paulinho da Força, a federal deputy and pre-candidate for the Senate in São Paulo, ruled out supporting Lula in the first round of the 2026 elections, though he did not define his party’s stance in the presidential race. He is attempting to build a centrist image to bolster his Senate candidacy, distancing himself from both Lula and Bolsonaro, but struggles with low popularity, registering only 0.5% in voter intention polls.

item.title: Lula diz que só vai sossegar quando preço do diesel parar de subir e lembra que guerra no Irã é de Trump
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva stated that his government will make every effort to curb the rise in diesel prices, emphasizing its inflationary impact on food. He also stressed that the war in Iran, promoted by the United States and Israel, must not harm Brazilians.

item.title: Nos bastidores do PL, vídeo de Eduardo Bolsonaro expõe tensão com Michelle
item.description: A video recorded by Eduardo Bolsonaro, in which he claims to be recording for his father Jair Bolsonaro, was interpreted as an indirect move to damage the image of Michelle Bolsonaro, seen as a moderate figure who influenced Alexandre de Moraes’ decision to grant house arrest to the former president. The PL denied sending the video to the ex-president, and party leader Valdemar Costa Neto said Eduardo acted by 'mistake' during CPAC in the U.S. The incident drew scrutiny from STF Minister Alexandre de Moraes, given the former president’s restricted internet access during house arrest.

item.title: Saiba quais ministros deixam os cargos para disputar as eleições
item.description: Lula government ministers have resigned their positions to comply with electoral incompatibility requirements ahead of the October 2026 elections. The legal deadline for stepping down is April 4, six months before the first round on October 4. The rule aims to prevent misuse of public resources and ensure fair competition among candidates.

item.title: Lula: Tomamos medidas para evitar aumento do diesel, mas governo passado vendeu distribuidoras
item.description: President Lula stated that the government took all possible measures to prevent diesel price increases, but blamed the privatization of fuel distributors under the Bolsonaro administration for blocking discounts from reaching consumers. He also criticized the UN Security Council’s permanent members for fueling wars instead of peace, referencing conflicts in Iran, Venezuela, and Cuba, and linked rising fuel prices to higher food costs.

item.title: Governo avalia usar dinheiro esquecido em bancos para reforçar programa de renegociação de dívidas
item.description: The Lula government is considering using R$10.5 billion in unclaimed bank funds, as reported by the Central Bank’s SVR, to strengthen the financing mechanism for a new debt renegotiation program aimed at reducing household debt burden (29.3% of income in February). The initiative, distinct from the 2023 Desenrola, may involve the FGO and focus on shifting debt to cheaper credit lines, though it lacks a final name or incentive details. The idea was previously proposed in 2024 to boost fiscal results but drew criticism from the Central Bank and opposition, who labeled it a 'confiscation'.

item.title: Lula deve enviar nesta terça ao Senado indicação de Messias ao STF
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva is set to send on Tuesday (31) the presidential message to the Senate formalizing the nomination of Attorney General Jorge Messias for a vacancy on the Supreme Federal Court (STF). The nomination was announced in November 2023 but was not submitted earlier due to lack of political support. Messias, 46, will fill the seat vacated by Luís Roberto Barroso, who retired in October 2025, and must undergo a hearing in the Senate's Constitution and Justice Committee (CCJ) before a plenary vote.

item.title: Messias promete ‘humildade’ e ‘diálogo’ com senadores
item.description: AGU Minister Jorge Messias said he will continue his Senate journey with humility and faith after the Planalto Palace confirmed his STF nomination, emphasizing dialogue and conciliation as the best ways to resolve conflicts and ensure stability. He pledged to seek understanding with all senators despite resistance from Senate President Davi Alcolumbre, which prompted President Lula to delay the formal submission of the nomination.

item.title: Em vídeo, secretária do PT faz brincadeira com Tarcísio de Freitas
item.description: Julia Köpf, national youth secretary of the Workers' Party (PT), made a joke in a video with Governor Tarcísio de Freitas, saying Fernando Haddad will 'surpass him' in the 2026 São Paulo elections. The video, posted on Instagram, has already reached 248,000 views and 2,895 comments, reflecting growing political debate in the state. According to an AtlasIntel/Estadão poll, Tarcísio has 53% approval and 45% disapproval among São Paulo voters.

item.title: “Tenho dúvidas sobre a candidatura do Caiado ainda”, diz Valdemar
item.description: Valdemar Costa Neto, president of the PL, said he has doubts about Ronaldo Caiado's (PSD) pre-candidacy for president, despite praising the former governor of Goiás. He believes the 2026 election runoff will be between President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva and an opposition candidate, without naming Caiado as a likely finalist.

item.title: Alcolumbre sabia que Lula faria o envio da mensagem com indicação de Messias ao STF, mas não foi comunicado sobre data
item.description: Lula sent the nomination of Jorge Messias for the STF to the Senate without informing Davi Alcolumbre of the exact date, although Alcolumbre knew it could happen in the coming days. The decision was made because the Planalto believes advancing the nomination will become harder as elections approach and Congress empties from June onward. Alcolumbre stated the timeline depends on his schedule and government negotiation, with no commitment to speed up or delay the process.

item.title: Fim da novela: Pacheco se filia ao PSB nesta quarta-feira
item.description: Rodrigo Pacheco announced he will leave the PSD to join the PSB and run for Governor of Minas Gerais in 2026, aiming to strengthen Lula's electoral base in the state. His party switch will take place this Wednesday, ending his tenure in the PSD and launching a new political phase aligned with the federal government. The news was released in an exclusive article for subscribers.

item.title: Lula cita alta dos combustíveis e manda novo recado a Trump
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva blamed Donald Trump for the rise in fuel prices in Brazil, linking the increase in oil costs to the U.S.-Israel war against Iran and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. He stated the federal government is taking all possible measures to curb diesel price increases and emphasized that Brazil should not bear the burden of foreign conflicts, declaring the war is Trump’s, not the Brazilian people’s.

item.title: Messias diz que vai trabalhar por votos no Senado 'com humildade e fé'
item.description: Jorge Messias, the Attorney General of the Union, stated that if approved for the STF, he will work in the Senate with humility and faith to secure the necessary votes for his appointment. He has waited over four months for President Lula to formally submit his name to Congress to begin the confirmation process.

item.title: Resistências de aliados travam substituição de Gleisi na articulação política, e governo avalia indicar interino
item.description: The Lula government is evaluating replacements for Gleisi Hoffmann in political coordination, with interim appointment of SRI executive secretary Marcelo Costa under consideration. José Guimarães (PT-CE) and Otto Alencar (PSD-BA) are mentioned as candidates, but Guimarães insists on running for Senate in Ceará and Alencar states he has no interest, despite allies noting his influence with Senate President Davi Alcolumbre to support Jorge Messias’ STF nomination. The initial plan to appoint Olavo Noleto was rejected by Lula in favor of a legislator with political experience, though a reversal on Noleto’s veto remains possible.

item.title: Pacheco comunica ao PSB que se filiará ao partido nesta quarta-feira em Brasília
item.description: Senator Rodrigo Pacheco (PSD-MG) announced he will join the PSB this Wednesday, April 1st, in Brasília. While the move is seen as a step toward a potential candidacy for governor of Minas Gerais, allies emphasize that the party switch is merely procedural and that the final decision on running will be made during party conventions between July and August.

item.title: Papo Antagonista: O ministério dos homens de Lula
item.description: In Papo Antagonista, state deputy Fabiana Bolsonaro discussed her protest against Erika Hilton, the confirmation of Geraldo Alckmin as Lula’s running mate, the 2026 elections, and the incident where Minister José Múcio spoke unaware his microphone was on. The conversation covered political tensions and controversial statements in the current landscape.

item.title: 'Fiz mais do que o Ministério das Mulheres todinho', diz Múcio em fala captada por microfone de reunião ministerial de Lula
item.description: Defense Minister José Múcio Monteiro claimed in a ministerial meeting that he had done 'more than the entire Ministry of Women' after his ministry was omitted from a presentation by Chief of Staff Rui Costa on Lula’s government achievements. Múcio said the omission was Costa’s error, who later apologized and acknowledged the comparison was inappropriate, noting the Ministry of Women has an advisory, not executive, role. The Defense Ministry highlighted progress in women’s inclusion, including the first female army general promotion and the launch of voluntary military service for women.

item.title: Messias diz que vai conversar com senadores em busca de votos ao STF após Lula confirmar envio de indicação: 'Momento de entendimento'
item.description: President Lula confirmed the submission of Jorge Messias' name, chief of the Attorney General's Office, to the Senate for a Supreme Court vacancy, four months after announcing it. Messias stated he will resume dialogue with senators to secure votes, emphasizing humility, faith, and commitment to dialogue and pacification. The move aims to overcome Senate President Davi Alcolumbre's resistance, who had favored Rodrigo Pacheco and delayed the process.

item.title: Crise entre poderes amplia riscos institucionais e desafia combate à corrupção no Brasil
item.description: Growing tensions between Brazil's branches of government, particularly between the Supreme Federal Court and Congress, weaken democratic institutions and undermine anti-corruption efforts. Experts warn of political fragmentation, rising parliamentary amendments lacking transparency, and international pressure on constitutional courts as key factors enabling corruption schemes.

item.title: A projeção de Fachin sobre a votação do Código de Ética para o STF
item.description: STF President Edson Fachin expects the Ethical Code for justices to be voted on in 2026, emphasizing that the discussion process is as important as the outcome. He argues the code’s most effective application is through ethical constriction — prompting self-reflection and behavioral correction — while acknowledging some justices prefer to delay the vote until after the elections to avoid politicizing the debate.

item.title: Após retotalização, deputado do PL deve assumir vaga de Bacellar na Alerj
item.description: The Rio de Janeiro Regional Electoral Court (TRE-RJ) indicated that Carlos Augusto Nogueira Pinto (PL) should assume the seat of Rodrigo Bacellar (União Brasil) in the Alerj after a vote recount, following Bacellar’s disqualification over fraud linked to the Ceperj case. Although Bacellar switched parties after being elected in 2022 under the PL, electoral rules dictate the seat returns to his original party. Carlos Augusto, a former deputy and current substitute, is set to take office after the appeal period, enabling the Alerj to elect a new president amid the state’s leadership vacuum after Governor Cláudio Castro’s resignation.

item.title: Ao vivo: Lula celebra 21 anos do Prouni e dos 14 anos da Lei de Cotas
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva attends the 'University with the Face of the Brazilian People' event in São Paulo, marking 21 years of Prouni and 14 years of the Racial Quotas Law. The gathering honors inclusion policies in higher education, featuring ministers, scholarship students, and social movement representatives. The government will unveil new initiatives, sign normative acts concerning Prouni and the National Hip Hop School Program (H2E), and release results from the Public Call for the National Network of Popular Preparatory Courses.

item.title: Prazos processuais ficam suspensos durante a Semana Santa
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) suspended operations from April 1 to 3 due to Holy Week, as established by TSE Ordinance No. 533/2025. Procedural deadlines beginning or ending during this period were automatically extended to April 6, the first business day after the holiday. The next plenary session of the TSE for judgments is scheduled for April 8, Tuesday.

item.title: Cotado para substituir Castro na eleição ao Senado, ex-chefe da Polícia Civil do Rio se filia ao PP
item.description: Former Rio de Janeiro Civil Police Chief Felipe Curi joined the PP after abandoning plans to run for governor under the PL. He is now being considered as a replacement for Claudio Castro in the Senate race, following Castro's eight-year ineligibility ruling by the TSE for abuse of political and economic power. The PP has already nominated other candidates for the gubernatorial ticket, including Márcio Canella for Senate and Rogério Lisboa as vice to Douglas Ruas.

item.title: Governo Lula é desaprovado por 56% em SP, diz AtlasIntel
item.description: President Lula's government is disapproved by 56% of voters in São Paulo, according to an AtlasIntel/Estado poll released on March 31, 2026. Disapproval is highest among men (61.4%), evangelicals (80.4%), voters who supported Tarcísio de Freitas in the 2022 runoff (98.4%), and those aged 45–59 (70.6%) and 16–24 (60.2%). The worst regional disapproval is in Presidente Prudente and Marília (75.6%), while the best performance is among voters with higher education, showing a technical tie (49.8% disapprove, 49.3% approve).

item.title: Senado entrega Diploma Bertha Lutz a 15 personalidades
item.description: The Senate awarded the Bertha Lutz Diploma to 15 personalities in honor of Women's Month, recognizing their contributions to women's rights and gender equality in Brazil. Senator Augusta Brito (PT-CE) emphasized that the award celebrates stories that have helped transform the country, while other lawmakers stressed the need for collective action — including men's involvement — to combat gender-based violence and inequality.

item.title: Fachin conversa com Moraes sobre encerramento do inquérito das fake news
item.description: Supreme Federal Court President Edson Fachin stated he is discussing with Minister Alexandre de Moraes the possibility of closing the fake news inquiry, which has been ongoing for over seven years. The remark was made on Tuesday (31) and suggests a potential shift in the proceedings, which investigate the spread of false news with possible electoral impact.

item.title: Lula sanciona aumento da licença-paternidade para 20 dias
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva signed a law gradually increasing paternity leave from 5 to 20 days, with increments in 2027 (10 days), 2028 (15 days), and 2029 (20 days). The policy applies to biological births and adoptions, includes paternity salary payments, and aims to advance gender equality by encouraging men’s involvement in childcare. Although a step forward, the 20-day leave remains significantly shorter than the four-month maternity leave, extendable under specific conditions.

item.title: Fachin defende aprovação de código de ética neste ano e diz que desvios devem gerar 'constrangimento' interno no STF
item.description: STF President Edson Fachin advocates for the approval of an ethics code for ministers by the end of 2026, drawing on international models, and argues that internal 'constraining' among justices should be the main mechanism for enforcing conduct. Minister Cármen Lúcia is drafting the proposal, to be reviewed in a public administrative session. Fachin also noted that the Court is internally discussing the possible closure of the fake news inquiry, while affirming its past importance for democracy.

item.title: José Dirceu aponta maioria no Senado para levar Messias ao STF
item.description: Former PT president José Dirceu stated that the Lula government already has Senate majority to approve Jorge Messias' nomination to the STF, citing his constitutional qualifications and the lack of historical precedent for rejecting a president's nominee. Lula will send the formal nomination message to the Senate on Tuesday, over four months after initially announcing Messias as his pick, despite initial opposition from Senate President Davi Alcolumbre, who had supported Senator Rodrigo Pacheco.

item.title: Lula teme impacto da guerra no preço dos alimentos e diz que governador precisam dividir a conta do aumento do diesel
item.description: President Lula expressed concern about the impact of the Middle East war on food and diesel prices, warning it could affect inflation and his popularity ahead of the 2026 elections. He urged governors to share the cost of a diesel subsidy, proposing an equal split between the federal government and states for R$1.20 per liter, with a monthly fiscal impact of R$1.5 billion. The Finance Minister said states are near unanimous in supporting the measure, which aims to reduce ICMS on diesel imports.

item.title: Moraes marca data de depoimento de Eduardo sobre atuação nos EUA por tarifaço e coação de ministros do STF
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes has scheduled Eduardo Bolsonaro's testimony for April 14 via videoconference in a case alleging coercion during judicial proceedings. The former deputy is accused of orchestrating sanctions against Brazilian authorities — including export tariffs and visa suspensions — to pressure the STF ahead of the ruling that condemned Jair Bolsonaro for attempted coup. Moraes ruled out summary dismissal and ordered the hearing after failed direct service attempts.

item.title: Código de conduta deve ser discutido ainda neste ano, diz Fachin
item.description: STF President Edson Fachin said the court's code of conduct should be discussed this year, though ministers disagree on timing and whether more time is needed for maturation. He noted that Minister Cármen Lúcia is drafting a preliminary version and reiterated his hope for approval during his term, possibly in 2026, despite some viewing the election year as an obstacle. Fachin also highlighted the lack of consensus on creating an ethics committee and its selection criteria.

item.title: Moraes determina que PGR se manifeste a respeito de fala de Eduardo sobre mostrar vídeo a Bolsonaro, em prisão domiciliar
item.description: Minister Alexandre de Moraes ordered the Prosecutor General's Office (PGR) to comment on Eduardo Bolsonaro's statement that he sent a video recorded in the U.S. to Jair Bolsonaro, allegedly violating the conditions of Bolsonaro's house arrest, which bans cell phones, social media, and external communications. Bolsonaro's defense denies any direct communication, claiming the video was a third-party statement with no involvement from the former president. The case is part of a proceeding where Eduardo is accused of coercion for acting with the U.S. government to impose sanctions on Brazilian authorities.

item.title: Lula confirma Alckmin como vice: quem são os nomes na disputa até agora
item.description: Lula confirmed Geraldo Alckmin as his running mate for the 2026 presidential election. The announcement was made during a ministerial meeting at the Palácio do Planalto, where 14 ministers were also cleared to run for office. Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado announced his pre-candidacy, advocating for broad amnesty, including for Lula, and stated his goal is to pacify Brazil and make the PT no longer a political option.

item.title: Regulamentação do FGTS como garantia do consignado sai em junho
item.description: Labor and Employment Minister Luiz Marinho announced that the regulation allowing the use of FGTS as collateral for the Worker's Credit will be published in June 2026. Launched in March 2025, the program has already exceeded R$101 billion in consigned loans with over 17 million contracts, enabling formal workers to use up to 10% of their FGTS balance as collateral, plus the 40% severance fine in case of unjust dismissal. The measure aims to lower interest rates for private-sector workers, responding to criticism of high rates observed in 2025.

item.title: Sancionada criação de cargos e funções comissionadas até 2028 no CNJ
item.description: Law 15.366, signed by President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, authorizes the gradual creation of effective, commissioned, and trust positions in the National Council of Justice (CNJ) between 2026 and 2028, including 50 judicial analysts, 70 judicial technicians, 20 commissioned positions, and 100 trust functions, funded by the CNJ’s own budget. The measure aims to align staffing with the CNJ’s expanded role in formulating and implementing judicial public policies, affecting the entire Judiciary.

item.title: Com apoio de Flávio Bolsonaro, ex-chefe da Polícia do Rio será candidato a deputado federal
item.description: Former Rio de Janeiro Civil Police chief Felipe Curi announced on Tuesday his affiliation with the Progressistas (PP) party and his candidacy for federal deputy in the 2026 elections, backed by Senator Flávio Bolsonaro. The exclusive subscriber-only article highlights the strengthening of Bolsonaro’s political base in Rio de Janeiro.

item.title: A ministros, Lula critica Flávio e diz temer inflação por crise do petróleo
item.description: President Lula criticized Flávio Bolsonaro during a farewell ministerial meeting, expressing concern over inflation caused by the oil crisis stemming from the Middle East conflict. He warned that rising prices could negatively impact the Brazilian economy.

item.title: Como aliados de Flávio leem entrada de Caiado na corrida presidencial
item.description: Allies of Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) are divided over Ronaldo Caiado’s entry into the presidential race: some believe he can help weaken Lula without directly attacking the Bolsonaro supporter base, while others see his candidacy as a threat by competing for the same conservative electorate. Despite the disagreement, there is a consensus that Caiado is unlikely to overcome the Lula-Flávio polarization and is widely viewed as an 'underdog' in the contest.

item.title: Agente envolvido em plano para tentar matar Lula, Alckmin e Moraes é exonerado da PF
item.description: The Ministry of Justice and Public Security dismissed federal police officer Wladimir Matos Soares, who was sentenced to 21 years in prison for involvement in the 'núcleo 3' of the coup plot aimed at keeping Jair Bolsonaro in power after the 2022 election defeat. The plot included assassination attempts against President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, Vice President Geraldo Alckmin, and Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes.

item.title: Sabatina de Messias ocorrerá no 'tempo de Davi' e não tem prazo definido ainda, diz presidente da CCJ
item.description: The sabatina of Minister Jorge Messias to the STF is likely to be delayed until the second semester, as it depends on Senate President Davi Alcolumbre’s agenda, who shows no urgency to advance the process. CCJ President Otto Alencar stated he will act only after receiving the nomination and that the timeline will follow Alcolumbre’s pace. The deadlock reflects the strained relationship between the Planalto and the Senate, with the nomination considered low priority ahead of the October municipal elections.

item.title: Ala do PSD avalia que Caiado se posicionou como plano B da direita e vê chance de Flávio murchar na campanha
item.description: A faction within the PSD views Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado as positioning himself as a 'Plan B' for right-wing voters by adopting Bolsonaro-aligned rhetoric, including a promise of amnesty for Jair Bolsonaro, anticipating that Flávio Bolsonaro may falter due to past controversies and internal strife within Bolsonarismo. While more centrist members of the party criticize these moves, they acknowledge the strategy as necessary to capture right-wing support should Flávio fail to withstand campaign pressures.

item.title: Os bilhões do Master e o racismo no Brasil
item.description: The article denounces that while Brazil's financial system treats billionaire losses by elites like Banco Master as administrative failures, movements for historical reparations for Black communities are criminalized as threats to stability. The Banco Master case, led by Daniel Vorcaro, involved a R$ 50 billion fraud, bribery of Central Bank officials, and coercion to evade oversight, while the state spent R$ 285 billion over 20 years on the 'war on drugs' — a racial control mechanism that incarcerates and kills predominantly Black people.

item.title: Lei reestrutura carreiras do serviço público federal
item.description: Law 15.367/2025 restructures federal public service careers, creating over 24,000 new permanent positions and benefiting more than 200,000 servants. The law introduces direct election of university rectors, incentives for technical-administrative staff in education, and establishes the Federal Institute of Sertão Paraibano, with an estimated budget impact of R$ 5.3 billion in 2026.
